home.title: Build enterprise products faster
home.description: A production-grade frontend foundation for scalable applications.

features.title: Built for production teams
features.subtitle: Opinionated architecture, shared design system, and performance budgets from day one.

architecture.title: Feature-based architecture
architecture.description: Clear ownership boundaries between routes, features, repositories, and application logic.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: Server dictionaries and client hooks with RTL-aware layout across locales.

performance.title: Performance by default
performance.description: Server-first rendering, static generation, and minimal client JavaScript per route.

ctaSection.title: Ready to ship your next landing?
ctaSection.description: Start from a maintainable foundation instead of rebuilding the same shell on every project.

about.title: About Enterprise
about.description: We help product teams ship reliable frontend platforms with clear architecture and long-term maintainability.

mission.title: Our mission
mission.body: Enterprise exists to reduce the cost of building production-grade web applications. We provide opinionated foundations so teams can focus on product value instead of reinventing infrastructure.

approach.title: How we work
approach.body: We favor server-first rendering, feature ownership, and proven reuse over speculative abstractions. Every architectural choice is documented and measurable.

values.title: What we optimize for
values.body: Maintainability, performance, accessibility, and developer experience are treated as product requirements—not afterthoughts.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: Please read these terms carefully before using the Enterprise landing experience.

acceptance.title: Acceptance
acceptance.body: By accessing this site you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

use.title: Permitted use
use.body: You may use this site for lawful purposes related to evaluating or adopting Enterprise products. You may not attempt to disrupt, reverse engineer, or misuse the service.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: The site is provided as-is without warranties. Enterprise is not liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from use of this site.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

blog.title: Blog
blog.description: Architecture notes and product updates from the Enterprise team.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Answers to common questions about the Enterprise landing stack and architecture.

stack.question: What technologies power this landing app?
stack.answer: Next.js 15 App Router, React Server Components, Tailwind CSS, and shared monorepo packages for UI, i18n, and theming.

i18n.question: How is internationalization handled?
i18n.answer: Locale-prefixed routes with server dictionaries for RSC and @enterprise/i18n for client components. RTL layout is handled by the design system.

data.question: How does data fetching work?
data.answer: Server application functions call repositories backed by injectable datasources. Mocks live in _mocks/; the API client handles HTTP only.

performance.question: What performance practices are enforced?
performance.answer: Static generation for marketing pages, minimal client JavaScript, explicit performance budgets, and server-first rendering by default.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.description: Tell us about your project and we will get back to you.
